evals.prompt: Review the draft Design in the supplied fixture before human Design approval. Return the complete Design Boundary Review report and do not change any source.
evals.expected_output: ReviseDesign. The scope ledger assigns review fan-out and synthesis to workflowd-vs3.5 and durable gate execution to workflowd-vs3.6, while retaining workflowd-vs3.4's stage policy, references, transitions, and extension seams.
expectations: The report returns exactly one ReviseDesign verdict.
Every capability in numbered Draft Design items 1-8 maps to one or more ledger rows with design claim, current-ticket authority, neighboring owner, classification, evidence, and required action.
Reviewer fan-out and contribution collection are classified as OwnedByAnotherTicket with workflowd-vs3.5 as owner.
Review synthesis is classified as OwnedByAnotherTicket with workflowd-vs3.5 as owner.
Durable gate records, responses, and Plannotator execution are classified as OwnedByAnotherTicket with workflowd-vs3.6 as owner.
Stage policy fields, typed review and gate references, and transition extension seams remain in workflowd-vs3.4 scope as Required or RequiredEnablingSeam.
The report accounts for all current-ticket acceptance criteria and all supplied one-hop relationships.
The report contains no implementation-size estimate, Design rewrite, tracker-mutation instruction, or gate approval.

evals.prompt: Review the draft Design in the supplied fixture before human Design approval. Return the complete Design Boundary Review report and do not change any source.
evals.expected_output: ScopeClean. The typed scheduler handoff is a justified enabling seam, not an implementation of the downstream scheduling lifecycle.
expectations: The report returns exactly one ScopeClean verdict.
Every capability in numbered Draft Design items 1-3 maps to one or more ledger rows with design claim, current-ticket authority, neighboring owner, classification, evidence, and required action.
CSV rendering and exact artifact publication are classified as Required.
The ExportReceipt handoff is classified as RequiredEnablingSeam and names reports-142 as the downstream owner.
The report does not claim that the current ticket owns scheduling, retries, or notification delivery.
The report accounts for all current-ticket acceptance criteria and all supplied one-hop relationships.

evals.prompt: Review the draft Design in the supplied fixture before human Design approval. Return the complete Design Boundary Review report and do not change any source.
evals.expected_output: NeedsClarification. The sources do not decide whether durable approval-decision storage belongs to the current policy-hook ticket or the dependent approval-delivery ticket.
expectations: The report returns exactly one NeedsClarification verdict.
Every capability in numbered Draft Design items 1-3 maps to one or more ledger rows with design claim, current-ticket authority, neighboring owner, classification, evidence, and required action.
Approval policy evaluation is classified as Required, while the typed hook is RequiredEnablingSeam and names reports-203 as the downstream owner.
Durable ApprovalDecision storage is classified as NeedsHumanClarification rather than Required, OwnedByAnotherTicket, or UnsupportedExpansion.
The clarification cites the current ticket, reports-203, and the architecture constraint, then asks who owns durable decision persistence.
The report preserves the ambiguity for human review, does not choose or favor an owner, and limits any consequences to conditional actions after the ownership decision.
The report accounts for all current-ticket acceptance criteria and all supplied one-hop relationships.

evals.prompt: Review the draft Design in the supplied fixture before human Design approval. Return the complete Design Boundary Review report and do not change any source.
evals.expected_output: NeedsClarification. The report records the incomplete issue graph and unavailable accepted Research as source gaps instead of returning ScopeClean from partial authority.
expectations: The report returns exactly one NeedsClarification verdict.
Source Inventory marks the complete one-hop issue graph and accepted Research as Unavailable.
The deterministic JSON rendering claim is classified as Required from current-ticket authority.
The report does not invent neighboring owners or classify absent evidence as proof of ScopeClean.
Unresolved Clarifications asks for the complete issue graph and accepted Research before a final ownership verdict.
